programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) - Maine PACE
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail elderly in community settings. It includes adult day health care, home care, and hospitalization to avoid nursing homes.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors aged 55+ who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: Full Medicare and Medicaid coverage of services; value depends on individual needs.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services Waiver Programs (e.g., MaineCare Waiver Services)
programs.what_it_does: These waivers provide in-home supportive services funded by MaineCare or state funds to elderly and disabled adults. Services aim to promote independence and delay institutionalization through assessments using the MED tool.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Elderly and disabled adults eligible via functional and financial assessment by Maximus.[1]
programs.benefit_value: In-home services like personal care, adult day, IHSP; co-payments may apply based on finances.[1]

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy food for low-income households. In Maine, administered by DHHS for seniors including shared household rules.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ and disabled individuals meeting income tests.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+ per month per person depending on income and household size.

programs.name: Low-Income Heating Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides funds to help low-income households pay heating costs, distributed by 11 Community Action Programs to fuel vendors. It assists with heating bills but does not cover all costs.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low and very low-income homeowners and renters.[4]
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,000+ per household annually based on income, household size, and fuel type.[4]

programs.name: Community Aging in Place Grant Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers no-cost home safety checks, minor repairs, and accessibility modifications for low-income elderly homeowners. Modeled after CAPABLE program to enable safe aging in place.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Age 55+, disabled, income ≤100% AMI, own or live in family-owned home in partner service areas.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free modifications up to several thousand dollars per home via 15 partner agencies.[2]

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP) - Maine SHIP
programs.what_it_does: Provides free counseling on Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Part D, and supplemental plans. Helps seniors choose and understand coverage options.
programs.who_its_for: All Maine Medicare beneficiaries, especially low-income seniors.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; potential savings of thousands on premiums/drugs.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often provided through Area Agencies on Aging.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week; suggested donation for 60+, free for lower incomes.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service work training for low-income seniors. Helps gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage pay at 20 hrs/week; work experience and support services.

programs.name: Maine Legal Services for the Elderly
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice and representation to seniors on health care, benefits, abuse, and consumer issues. Covers Medicare, MaineCare, housing, and guardianship defense.[4][6]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services valued at thousands in attorney fees.

programs.name: Legal Services for Maine Elders (LSE)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free legal advice and representation for seniors on health care, Medicare, MaineCare, Social Security, abuse, guardianship, and consumer issues. Serves as a helpline for economically needy elderly.[2][5]
programs.who_its_for: Socially and economically needy Maine residents aged 60+.[2][5]
programs.whats_unique: Maine-specific nonprofit providing targeted free legal aid for elder issues, integrated with state aging network.[2][5]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services; prevents financial loss from abuse, denials, or poor planning.[2][5]
